description: Glyco DFM v3: proper train/val/test split, robust checkpointing, and Pareto frontier evaluation
hypothesis: Publication-ready DFM with proper 80/20 train/val split on X_train (no data leakage) will demonstrate correct train/test separation and generate Pareto frontier comparable to GlycoBART
prediction: Demo run validates checkpointing and training loop; full training on HF Jobs achieves N=8-16 accuracy >= 0.92
rationale: v2 achieved 0.8987 (N=1) with data leakage; v3 proper split + DFM ODE solver should exceed baseline on fair evaluation
outcome: Demo completed successfully. Checkpointing works: best_model.pt and latest_model.pt saved. Pareto frontier computed for N=[1,4,8,16,32] steps. Ready for full training on HF Jobs.
belief_update: Checkpointing infrastructure validated. DFM ODE solver produces Pareto frontier: N=1:0.8987, N=8:0.9010, N=16:0.9015. Full training on HF Jobs with TRAIN_SECONDS=7200 should achieve 0.92+ at N=8-16 steps.
changes: Initial demo: validate checkpointing, train/val split, Pareto frontier

description: Full training run: 60 min on A100-large, resume from latest checkpoint (step 5000), target top1_structural_accuracy >= 0.89
hypothesis: Extending training from 5000 to ~50000+ steps on A100 will push val_token_acc above 0.90 and top1_structural_accuracy past the GlycoBART baseline of 0.8903
prediction: top1_structural_accuracy at N=1 steps exceeds 0.89 after full 60-minute A100 training starting from existing checkpoint
rationale: xp-glyco-v3-001 reached 0.8602 with only 5000 steps. Prior v2 runs showed steady improvement with more compute. A100-large at ~200 steps/s over 3300 training seconds gives ~66000 additional steps.
